title: Hieracium glabratoides
taxonomy: Hieracium glabratoides is classified as a species of plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a type of hawkweed, which is a group of plants within the genus Hieracium. The genus Hieracium is classified as part of the tribe Lactuceae, which is a group of plants that share similar characteristics. The family Asteraceae, to which Hieracium glabratoides belongs, is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the Asteraceae family.

intro: Hieracium glabratoides, also known as smooth hawkweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion-like Hawkweed genus. Traditionally, Hieracium glabratoides has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, skin issues, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's bioactive constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Hieracium glabratoides is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to support the body's natural defenses and promote overall well-being.
